name: scenario_945172_financial_services_municipal_bond_covenant_mesa_lib_eq
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q2 2026: 6 critical issues have surfaced over 48 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Financial Services. The immediate incident is a municipal bond covenant breach risk. The trigger is that financial covenants are at risk, and accounting fixes may hide operational failures rather than resolve them. The board has pre-approved up to $10.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as liberty vs equality: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a 72-hour records production deadline. Boundaries: Public communications must distinguish verified facts from hypotheses. All exceptions require a written ticket with a reason code. No new data sources may be added during the incident window. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Program Chair Hector Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Program Chair Hector Vasquez, coordinating the Financial Services response at Mesa Financial Services Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an operational memo that withstands regulator questioning: assumptions stated, risks assigned, rollback triggers defined.

agents.name: Labor Representative Ansel Chandra
agents.identity: I am Labor Representative Ansel Chandra, coordinating the Financial Services response at Mesa Financial Services Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with independent log capture, a reversible kill-switch, and two-person sign-off for overrides; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Program Chair Hector Vasquez must choose an authorization approach for this Financial Services decision. What does Program Chair Hector Vasquez do?
